channel.title: NPR Topics: Religion
channel.description: NPR's stories on U.S. and world religion, spirituality, ethics, and moral issues affecting society and culture. Subscribe to NPR Religion RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How the Trump administration uses the Bible to justify military invasions and immigration raids
item.description: President Trump isn't much of a Bible-quoter, but some members of his administration are, and they have used Scripture to frame controversial policies as justified by the Good Book.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump isn't much of a Bible-quoter, but some members of his administration are, and they have used Scripture to frame controversial policies as justified by the Good Book. 

(Image credit: Pool photo)
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Pope Leo warns that AI is becoming a new test of human dignity, work and power
item.description: In an encyclical, Pope Leo says AI poses a threat to human dignity.
item.content:encoded: In an encyclical, Pope Leo says AI poses a threat to human dignity.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: Pope Leo takes aim at big tech in sweeping encyclical on AI
item.description: "Magnifica Humanitas" tackles the social, economic and political challenges associated with artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
"Magnifica Humanitas" tackles the social, economic and political challenges associated with artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: Alessandra Tarantino)
item.dc:creator: Claire Giangrave

item.title: Exploring the evolving relationship between church and state in America
item.description: This week, NPR explores the relationship between church and state through the lens of people who believe the United States is, and should continue to be, a Christian nation.
item.content:encoded: This week, NPR explores the relationship between church and state through the lens of people who believe the United States is, and should continue to be, a Christian nation.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Muslims begin the annual Hajj against a backdrop of war concerns
item.description: More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.
item.content:encoded:
More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.

(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pope Leo's first encyclical will address AI and 'magnificent humanity'
item.description: Pope Leo plans to release an encyclical on "safeguarding the human person in the time of artificial intelligence." NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to University of Notre Dame professor Meghan Sullivan.
item.content:encoded: Pope Leo plans to release an encyclical on "safeguarding the human person in the time of artificial intelligence." NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to University of Notre Dame professor Meghan Sullivan.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Iran war disrupts Mecca pilgrimage plans for some American Muslims
item.description: Making the pilgrimage to Mecca is a once-in-a-lifetime experience and a sacred obligation for many Muslims. But the ongoing Iran war is making some American Muslims reconsider their plans.
item.content:encoded: Making the pilgrimage to Mecca is a once-in-a-lifetime experience and a sacred obligation for many Muslims. But the ongoing Iran war is making some American Muslims reconsider their plans.
item.dc:creator: Nargis Rahman

item.title: Catholic Diocese fights federal government's effort to take possession of 'holy site'
item.description: The federal government has filed a lawsuit to seize land to build a border wall, sparking a fight over religious freedom. The Catholic Diocese owns the Mount Cristo Rey property it calls a holy site.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has filed a lawsuit to seize land to build a border wall, sparking a fight over religious freedom. The Catholic Diocese owns the Mount Cristo Rey property it calls a holy site.
item.dc:creator: Angela Kocherga

item.title: Israel allows women to take rabbinic test for 1st time, but not become Orthodox rabbis
item.description: After years of court battles, Israel will allow women to take the exams men take to become rabbis. It is a step toward expanding women's leadership roles in Orthodox Judaism.
item.content:encoded: After years of court battles, Israel will allow women to take the exams men take to become rabbis. It is a step toward expanding women's leadership roles in Orthodox Judaism.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: As Hajj nears, Pakistan pilgrims face uncertainty amid regional conflict
item.description: As millions of Muslims prepare for the annual Hajj pilgrimage to Mecca, Pakistan is sending one of the largest groups amid added uncertainty from the war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: As millions of Muslims prepare for the annual Hajj pilgrimage to Mecca, Pakistan is sending one of the largest groups amid added uncertainty from the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles
